experience.title: Course Assistant
bullets: Instruct first-year engineering students in the First-Year Engineering Computing lab course by assisting with homework problems, answering questions during lab time, and guiding course assignments

experience.title: Sales Associate
bullets: Provide guests with food and any other assistance in a timely and respectful manner
Stock and organize storage and inventory of the ice cream and additional material
Manage the cash register and all customer transactions while assuring accuracy and correct payments

education.institution: Binghamton University, SUNY, Thomas J. Watson College of Engineering and Applied Science
education.degree: Bachelor of Science in Industrial and Systems Engineering

projects.name: Global Issues Project: Solar Panel
bullets: Research energy generation systems, solar panels, and solar system designs to specifications based on a set of requirements to model a fully functional solar panel for implementation using CAD software
Manage a team of seven other engineers in brainstorming, scheduling, and task delegation to present designs for solar panels at an engineering exposition for faculty, students, and the public

projects.name: Arduino Project: Simon Says
bullets: Generated a functioning version of the game 'Simon Says' and presented the game along with a poster at the Watson Expo with over 400 students and faculty in attendance
Soldered wire connections to a printed circuit board from the breadboard model design prototype
Coded in C for an LCD score counter modification where the score increased by 1 for each successful input
Modeled a themed case for the game in SOLIDWORKS CAD and 3D printed the design for display at the exposition

projects.name: Reverse Engineering Project: Waffle Maker
bullets: Collaborated with a team of four to reverse engineer and re-design a waffle maker through analysis, deconstruction, and a CAD-modeled re-design outlining possible improvements
Constructed a 24-page technical report including Gantt Charts, evaluation matrices, CAD models, and technical revisions, which was presented to faculty and staff
Actively participate in professional development workshops to enhance skills such as resume writing, interview techniques, and networking
Contribute to discussions in chapter meetings and activities aimed at promoting the goals of the chapter
Build connections with peers, mentors, and industry professionals through active participation in events
Attend sessions focused on career advancement, technical skills, and leadership development
Volunteer in outreach programs to inspire and mentor young women interested in pursuing engineering
